items.source: Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau — Press releases
items.title: FSTB and HKMA to further unlock potential of distributed ledger technology in Hong Kong's fixed income market

sources.name: Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau — Press releases

items.summary: The Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau and the Hong Kong Monetary Authority announced further measures to expand the use of distributed ledger technology in Hong Kong's fixed-income market.
items.so_what: Signals a continuing official push to make tokenised debt mainstream in Hong Kong — expect follow-on guidance touching settlement, custody and record-keeping controls.

items.source: European Banking Authority — News
items.title: ​The European Banking Authority consults on a draft methodology for setting fines under the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA)

sources.name: European Banking Authority — News

items.summary: The European Banking Authority is consulting on a draft methodology for setting fines on issuers it supervises under the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation.
items.so_what: A concrete preview of how penalties under the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation will be sized — a useful benchmark when gauging the EU enforcement exposure of stablecoin and token issuers.

items.source: European Banking Authority — News
items.title: ​The EBA updates validation rules for supervisory reporting ​

sources.name: European Banking Authority — News

items.summary: The European Banking Authority published updated validation rules for supervisory reporting, flagging corrections reporting institutions should apply.
items.so_what: Routine, but worth passing to regulatory-reporting teams handling EU entities, including MiCA-related returns.

items.source: US Securities and Exchange Commission — Press releases
items.title: SEC, CFTC Seek Public Comment to Further Clarify and Harmonize Derivatives Product Definitions

sources.name: US Securities and Exchange Commission — Press releases

items.summary: The US Securities and Exchange Commission and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission jointly asked for public comment on clarifying and harmonising their derivatives product definitions, which shape which agency oversees digital-asset derivatives.
items.so_what: Where the definitional lines land will affect how crypto derivatives are regulated and surveilled in the US — worth tracking for jurisdictional and market-conduct questions.

items.source: Federal Reserve — Press releases
items.title: Federal Reserve Board requests comment on proposal to require certain payment stablecoin issuers to maintain an effective customer identification program

sources.name: Federal Reserve — Press releases

items.summary: The Federal Reserve proposed requiring certain payment-stablecoin issuers to maintain an effective customer identification programme, with a comment period now open.
items.so_what: US regulators are extending core know-your-customer obligations to stablecoin issuers — a template other regimes may mirror and a direct input for onboarding-standard comparisons.

items.source: Global Digital Finance (GDF) — News
items.title: GDF and Block Release Landmark Report on Self-Custody Wallets

sources.name: Global Digital Finance (GDF) — News

items.summary: Global Digital Finance and Block published a joint report on self-custody wallets and their place in the digital-asset ecosystem.
items.so_what: Industry positioning on self-custody feeds directly into travel-rule and unhosted-wallet risk debates — useful context when framing policy responses.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: HKMA demonstrates potential of tokenisation to corporate treasury community

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority showcased tokenisation use cases to the corporate treasury community, continuing its promotion of tokenised money and assets.
items.so_what: Growing official sponsorship means corporate clients may raise tokenised-product questions sooner than product roadmaps assume.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: HKMA establishes Tokenised Bond Expert Group

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority established a Tokenised Bond Expert Group to advise on developing Hong Kong's tokenised bond market.
items.so_what: Another institutional building block for Hong Kong tokenisation — expect standards and market-practice outputs that compliance frameworks will need to track.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: SPM CRP-1: update on cryptoassets classification conditions

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority updated Supervisory Policy Manual module CRP-1 on the conditions for classifying banks' cryptoasset exposures.
items.so_what: Classification drives the capital treatment of bank crypto exposures — prudential and risk teams should re-check current classifications against the update.

items.source: Securities and Futures Commission — Circulars
items.title: Circular to licensed corporations, SFC-licensed virtual asset service providers and associated entities — Enhanced cybersecurity measures to address evolving risks arising from artificial intelligence-enabled cyberattacks

sources.name: Securities and Futures Commission — Circulars

items.summary: The Securities and Futures Commission directed licensed firms, including virtual-asset service providers, to strengthen cybersecurity controls against AI-enabled attacks.
items.so_what: Expect supervisory questions on AI-attack readiness at virtual-asset platforms — worth confirming vendor and platform assessments cover the circular's expectations.

items.source: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases
items.title: Project Agorá shows how tokenisation can improve wholesale cross-border payments; work will advance to real-value testing

sources.name: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases

items.summary: The Bank for International Settlements reported that Project Agorá demonstrated how tokenisation can improve wholesale cross-border payments, with work advancing to real-value testing.
items.so_what: Central-bank-grade tokenised settlement moving to real value keeps correspondent-banking modernisation on the strategic agenda.

items.source: Securities and Futures Commission — Circulars
items.title: Circular on provision of Relevant Stablecoin service by virtual asset trading platforms and licensed corporations

sources.name: Securities and Futures Commission — Circulars

items.summary: The Securities and Futures Commission issued a circular on how virtual asset trading platforms and licensed corporations may provide services in 'relevant stablecoins' issued by licensed issuers.
items.so_what: Defines the permitted distribution channel for Hong Kong-licensed stablecoins — a key input for product-approval and intermediary-oversight checklists.

items.source: Securities and Futures Commission — Circulars
items.title: Circular on SFC-authorised funds with exposure to virtual assets

sources.name: Securities and Futures Commission — Circulars

items.summary: The Securities and Futures Commission set out requirements for SFC-authorised funds seeking exposure to virtual assets.
items.so_what: Widens the regulated fund wrapper for virtual-asset exposure — fund-distribution and suitability controls should reflect the updated conditions.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Guidance on Offering of Financing for Virtual Asset Dealing, Shared Order Book, and Client Virtual Asset Withdrawals

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority issued guidance covering banks offering financing for virtual-asset dealing, shared order books, and client virtual-asset withdrawals.
items.so_what: Directly shapes what banking services can be offered around virtual-asset dealing — relevant to product sign-off and credit-risk policy for digital-asset clients.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Virtual asset-related activities in relation to relevant stablecoins issued by licensed stablecoin issuers

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority set expectations for banks' virtual-asset activities involving relevant stablecoins issued by licensed stablecoin issuers.
items.so_what: Clarifies the banking-side perimeter for handling Hong Kong-licensed stablecoins — a foundation for onboarding and monitoring rules around issuer and distribution relationships.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Updated Guidance on Provision of Custodial Services for Digital Assets

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority updated its guidance on banks providing custodial services for digital assets.
items.so_what: Custody control frameworks mapped to the earlier guidance need refreshing — safekeeping, key-management and client-asset segregation expectations may have moved.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Relevant Stablecoin-related Activities

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority issued a circular on banks' involvement in relevant stablecoin-related activities.
items.so_what: Part of the Hong Kong stablecoin regime rollout — read alongside the Securities and Futures Commission's distribution circular when mapping permissible activities.

items.source: Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau — Press releases
items.title: FSTB and SFC publish consultation conclusions on legislative proposals for regulating virtual asset advisory and management services

sources.name: Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau — Press releases

items.summary: The Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau and the Securities and Futures Commission published consultation conclusions on legislative proposals to regulate virtual-asset advisory and asset-management services.
items.so_what: Confirms Hong Kong will extend licensing beyond trading platforms — firms advising on or managing virtual-asset portfolios should begin gap-assessing against the concluded framework.

items.source: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases
items.title: Basel Committee agrees to publish report on information and communication technology risk management, progresses cryptoasset targeted review, considers targeted updates on liquidity risk principles

sources.name: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases

items.summary: The Basel Committee agreed to publish a report on information and communication technology risk management and progressed its targeted review of the cryptoasset prudential standard.
items.so_what: The cryptoasset-standard review could recalibrate the capital treatment of bank crypto exposures — a slow-burn item for prudential watchers.

items.source: Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau — Press releases
items.title: Inland Revenue (Amendment) (Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework and Amended Common Reporting Standard) Bill 2026 to be gazetted

sources.name: Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau — Press releases

items.summary: Hong Kong announced that a bill implementing the OECD Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework and the amended Common Reporting Standard will be gazetted.
items.so_what: The Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework brings mandatory tax-transparency reporting for crypto — data, onboarding and reporting pipelines will need to identify in-scope crypto assets and users.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: Beware of tokens purported to be associated with licensed stablecoin issuers

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority warned the public about tokens falsely claiming association with licensed stablecoin issuers.
items.so_what: Fraudsters are already free-riding on the licensing regime's credibility — a scenario worth adding to scam-typology and client-communication playbooks.

items.source: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases
items.title: CPMI-IOSCO assesses that the United Kingdom has implemented the Principles for financial market infrastructures for two FMI types, but recommends some improvements

sources.name: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases

items.summary: An assessment by the global payments and securities standard-setters (CPMI-IOSCO) found the UK has implemented the Principles for Financial Market Infrastructures for two financial market infrastructure types, while recommending improvements.
items.so_what: Background for financial market infrastructure due diligence; limited direct digital-asset impact.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: Granting of stablecoin issuer licences

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority announced the granting of stablecoin issuer licences under Hong Kong's new regime.
items.so_what: Licensed issuers now exist — screening lists, product approvals and counterparty assessments should distinguish licensed HK issuers from unlicensed lookalikes.

items.source: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) — Press releases
items.title: Treasury Proposes Rule to Implement the GENIUS Act’s Requirements to Counter Illicit Finance

sources.name: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) — Press releases

items.summary: The US Treasury proposed a rule implementing the GENIUS Act's requirements to counter illicit finance in the payment-stablecoin sector.
items.so_what: Sets the anti-money-laundering (AML/CFT) baseline for US stablecoin issuers — a key comparator for the Hong Kong and Singapore regimes and for assessing US issuer counterparties.

items.source: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases
items.title: Basel III liquidity indicators increase slightly while risk-based capital and leverage ratios are stable for large internationally active banks, latest Basel III monitoring exercise shows

sources.name: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases

items.summary: The Basel Committee's latest monitoring exercise shows liquidity indicators slightly up and capital ratios stable for large internationally active banks.
items.so_what: Context only; no direct digital-asset action required.

items.source: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases
items.title: Governors and Heads of Supervision welcome progress to implement Basel III and discuss elements of the Basel Committee's work programme

sources.name: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases

items.summary: Governors and Heads of Supervision welcomed Basel III implementation progress and discussed the Basel Committee's work programme.
items.so_what: Work-programme signals often preview future cryptoasset-standard attention — one to skim, not action.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: Hong Kong Monetary Authority, Shanghai Data Bureau, and National Technology Innovation Center for Blockchain sign MoU to strengthen Shanghai-Hong Kong cooperation in digitised cargo trade and finance

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority, Shanghai Data Bureau and China's National Technology Innovation Center for Blockchain signed a memorandum of understanding on Shanghai–Hong Kong cooperation in digitised cargo trade and finance.
items.so_what: A rare formal Hong Kong–mainland blockchain channel — relevant to trade-finance digitisation and cross-border data-flow considerations.

items.source: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases
items.title: Basel Committee discusses recent market developments and targeted review of cryptoasset standard

sources.name: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases

items.summary: The Basel Committee discussed recent market developments and its targeted review of the cryptoasset prudential standard.
items.so_what: Keep watching: changes here flow straight into the capital treatment of bank crypto exposures.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: Designation of Domestic Systemically Important Authorized Institutions

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority published its updated designation of domestic systemically important authorised institutions.
items.so_what: Prudential housekeeping; minimal direct digital-asset impact.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Cryptoassets standard: gazettal of revised SPM modules and code of practice

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: Hong Kong gazetted revised Supervisory Policy Manual modules and a code of practice implementing the cryptoassets prudential standard.
items.so_what: The Basel cryptoasset standard is now hard law in Hong Kong — capital, disclosure and booking-model implications for any bank crypto exposure.

items.source: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases
items.title: Basel Committee publishes more details on the 2025 assessment of global systemically important banks

sources.name: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee — Press releases

items.summary: The Basel Committee published further details of its 2025 assessment of global systemically important banks.
items.so_what: Reference material for prudential teams; no digital-asset-specific action.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Cryptoassets Standard: SPM Modules, Code of Practice, Disclosure Templates and Tables, and Banking Returns

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority issued the cryptoassets-standard package: Supervisory Policy Manual modules, code of practice, disclosure templates and banking returns.
items.so_what: The operational toolkit for the new prudential regime — reporting and disclosure teams should schedule the new returns and templates.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: HKMA announces the new phase of Project Ensemble to support real-value transactions in tokenised deposits and digital assets

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority launched a new phase of Project Ensemble supporting real-value transactions in tokenised deposits and digital assets.
items.so_what: Tokenised deposits moving to real value in Hong Kong — banks participating or competing will need settlement, custody and monitoring controls ready.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: HKMA completes e-HKD Pilot Programme and outlines future direction of e-HKD

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority completed its e-HKD pilot programme and set out the future direction for a potential retail digital Hong Kong dollar.
items.so_what: No launch decision, but the direction-setting shapes long-term payments and CBDC planning assumptions.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Supplemental joint circular on intermediaries’ virtual asset-related activities

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Securities and Futures Commission and the Hong Kong Monetary Authority issued a supplemental joint circular on intermediaries' virtual-asset-related activities.
items.so_what: Updates the core rulebook for banks and brokers distributing virtual-asset products in Hong Kong — compliance manuals citing the earlier joint circular need a version check.

items.source: Wolfsberg Group — News & publications
items.title: The Wolfsberg Group issues guidance on the provision of banking services to stablecoin issuers

sources.name: Wolfsberg Group — News & publications

items.summary: The Wolfsberg Group issued guidance for banks on providing banking services to stablecoin issuers.
items.so_what: The de facto industry benchmark for onboarding and monitoring stablecoin-issuer clients — a ready-made reference for policy and risk-appetite reviews.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Rules on cryptoassets standard and other updates

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority notified banks of new rules implementing the cryptoassets standard and related updates.
items.so_what: Companion notification to the gazettal — confirm implementation timelines against the rules as made.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: Joint Statement by the HKMA and the SFC on Stablecoin-related Market Movements

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority and the Securities and Futures Commission issued a joint statement responding to stablecoin-related market movements.
items.so_what: Regulators publicly managing stablecoin market froth — a useful signal of supervisory temperature around the new regime.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases
items.title: Implementation of regulatory regime for stablecoin issuers

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Press releases

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority announced the implementation of Hong Kong's regulatory regime for stablecoin issuers.
items.so_what: The regime is live — issuer licensing status becomes a hard gating item for any stablecoin-related product, client or listing decision in Hong Kong.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Gazettal of rules: cryptoassets standard and other updates

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: Hong Kong gazetted rules on the cryptoassets standard and related updates.
items.so_what: Legal-effect confirmation of the prudential package; align capital-reporting timelines accordingly.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Provision of Staking Services for Virtual Assets from Custodial Services

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority issued a circular on banks providing staking services for virtual assets from custodial services.
items.so_what: Defines how far Hong Kong bank custodians can go into staking — product and operational-risk assessments should track the stated conditions.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Research Paper on Distributed Ledger Technology in the Financial Sector

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority published a research paper on distributed ledger technology in the financial sector, covering opportunities and challenges.
items.so_what: Signals where official thinking on distributed ledger technology adoption is heading; background reading rather than action.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Circular Issued by the Securities and Futures Commission on Deficiencies and Substandard Conduct Noted in the Management of Private Funds and Discretionary Accounts (PDF File, 142.8 KB)

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Securities and Futures Commission circulated findings on deficiencies and substandard conduct in the management of private funds and discretionary accounts.
items.so_what: Supervisory expectations for private-fund managers, including those running virtual-asset strategies — worth a read-across for asset-management oversight.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Supervisory Incubator for Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) (PDF File, 172.4 KB)

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority launched a Supervisory Incubator for distributed ledger technology to support banks adopting the technology safely.
items.so_what: A structured channel to derisk distributed ledger technology projects with the regulator — teams planning tokenisation builds should factor it into engagement plans.

items.source: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance
items.title: Cryptoasset disclosure requirements and standard amendments (PDF File, 189.2 KB)

sources.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority — Circulars & guidance

items.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority issued cryptoasset disclosure requirements and standard amendments for banks.
items.so_what: New disclosure line items for bank crypto exposures — finance and reporting teams should map data availability early.
